vulnerability.reason: Simplicity bias causing under-scrutiny of prompts appearing benign is present, though relevance bias is also mentioned.
vulnerability.reference: Simplicity bias in ranking leads to under-scrutiny of harmful prompts that appear simple or benign.
vulnerability.generated: Ranking-based harmfulness evaluators systematically misjudge due to embedded simplicity and relevance biases, enabling jailbreak prompts to appear less harmful.

impact.reason: Damage type is generalized to high-risk content and real-world harm, and explicit affected party (users) is missing.
impact.reference: Harmful prompts bypass guard, generating toxic, biased, or manipulative content for users.
impact.generated: LLMs bypass safety mechanisms and generate high-risk content, causing real-world harm and undermining trust in AI safeguards.

dose_response.reason: Causal logic matches: cognitive biases in ranking evaluation lead to misjudgment, bypass, and harmful content generation.
dose_response.reference: Ranking-based harmfulness evaluation with cognitive biases introduces systematic misassessment, enabling harmful prompts to bypass guard models deployed by LLM developers, leading to toxic, biased, or manipulative content generation.
dose_response.generated: Ranking-based harmfulness evaluation using cognitive biases, deployed in LLM guard models, triggers systematic misjudgment that makes jailbreak prompts appear less harmful, leading to LLMs bypassing safeguards and generating high-risk content, causing real-world harm and undermining trust.
